title: Planet Labs Margin Expansion on Revenue Mix Shift and Automation
text: Planet Labs has achieved significant gross margin improvement, expanding by 1,930 basis points over four years. The company's margin expansion stems from transition from hardware-like, usage-based revenue toward higher-margin, subscription-based data products and analytics services. This shift toward recurring contracts, particularly with government and enterprise clients, reduces incremental delivery costs. Improvements include a stable satellite fleet reducing launch frequency, vertical integration lowering per-unit costs, and increased automation supporting disciplined cost management. Management expects cost of revenues to rise due to investments in delivery capabilities and new product development. However, the company targets gross margins of approximately 50-52%, banking on subscription revenue growth and analytics offerings to drive economies of scale. Despite strong operational improvement, Planet Labs trades at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 22.15, significantly above both its three-year median (3.65) and industry average (2.14), suggesting the stock may be priced for perfection.
